parsed_review.summary: CP00-407 is a descriptor-only, risk-class-A payments-core pack covering 10 units (RP13.P06.M05.S19-RP13.P06.M06.S06): the M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail (4 test rows) and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (6 fixture rows). The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly FALSE with flag payments_core_p06_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor_verified at the descriptor layer; that descriptor-verified claim is fully supported by source, contract, tests, and validator. Verified: (1) coverage/distribution (4 test / 1 security_audit / 5 implementation; M05:4 + M06:6) is consistent across baseline, manifest, contract cp407_requirements, registry binding, plan, and validator; (2) live-queue alignment - plan live_state is CP00-406 just-closed with next_subphase RP13.P06.M05.S19, and CP00-407 is the first pending queue pack starting exactly there (deviation_from_plan:false, matching ledger SHA), with contract historical_pack_bindings chaining CP00-406->CP00-407->CP00-408; (3) all 7 required public exports present and wired (index.js wildcard re-export explains its unmodified status); (4) descriptor-only/no-write boundaries - every case-set row is descriptor_only:true/runtime_execution:false/raw_payload_included:false and freezeCp407Result stamps 30+ no-write/no-runtime/no-leak flags, with no leaky row-extras; (5) authority boundary - claude_is_final_approval:false, read_only with only Read/Grep/Glob, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false, enforced by tests and validator deepEqual; (6) Hermes used only as a deterministic evidence-packet descriptor, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, not embedded in product runtime; (7) review-evidence validity - readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON), prompt 2166<25600 bytes, read_tools_used, no forbidden tools, no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-shaped output. Downstream evidence files (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are absent as expected since they are produced after this read_only_claude_run step in the hardening sequence. One cosmetic, non-blocking P3: changed-file-scope.json double-lists the pack's own evidence dir as both active-untracked and unrelated-preserved. No P0/P1/P2 issues; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-lists pack evidence dir as both active and unrelated
findings.details: The pack's own evidence directory docs/closeout-packs/cp00-407/ (and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md) appear simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133, in-scope for this pack) and in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 135-145, labeled unrelated). The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json lines 545-553) correctly omits both and lists only the genuinely unrelated dirty files (the v2.0 docx, the ldip invalid-attempt json, and the workbook matter files). This is a metadata-labeling inconsistency between two review artifacts; the pack's active implementation refs (the 8 payments source files) and evidence set are otherwise correctly scoped, and both dispositions preserve the file (no revert), so there is no functional impact on scope handling.
findings.recommendation: Align changed-file-scope.json with the manifest by classifying docs/closeout-packs/cp00-407/ (and new-session-handoff.md) solely under untracked_implementation_files and removing them from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, so the pack's own evidence dir is not also labeled unrelated.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic metadata inconsistency. Does not affect the descriptor-level production_ready claim, the no-write/no-real-data boundaries, the read-only Claude authority boundary, or any validation gate. Safe to close CP00-407; optionally tidy the scope artifact in a later pack.
